tldr: Replace a JS/TS framework flag SDK with a Java/Spring enum-based toggle library — usually triggered by a stack shift from Next.js to the JVM.
why_switch: Your platform consolidated on Java/Spring and the Next.js-centric SDK no longer fits
You want type-safe Java enum flags evaluated in-process
You'd rather drop the SaaS provider sitting behind Flags SDK
You prefer a long-standing, conservative library with a built-in admin console
You want zero external dependencies in the flag-evaluation path
what_to_export: Flag keys defined via flag() in your Next.js/SvelteKit code
Targeting/rule definitions from the backing provider
User attribute schemas used in rules
Existing A/B test setup tied to Vercel previews
Audit history from the backing provider
export_method: Flags SDK itself is a thin framework layer; flag rules live in the backing provider (LaunchDarkly, Statsig, ConfigCat, Flipt, etc.). Use that provider's native export tooling to dump rules and segments, and read the flag() declarations in your code to enumerate keys and defaults. Consult the official docs of both Flags SDK and your provider for current export formats.
import_method: Add the Togglz dependency to your Java/Spring Boot project, declare flags as values of an enum implementing Togglz's Feature interface, and pick a StateRepository (in-memory, JDBC, Redis, etc.). Translate each provider rule to a Togglz ActivationStrategy with the appropriate parameters. Enable the Togglz admin console for runtime management. Refer to the official Togglz documentation for current setup steps.

concept_mapping.note: Convert string keys to valid Java identifiers
Backend moves from external SaaS/Flipt to in-process JVM
Built-in strategies cover common cases; custom rules need a custom strategy
Latency profile and caching assumptions change
Togglz is on/off-first; emulate variants in app code or pick another tool
Experimentation must be handled outside Togglz

gotchas: Togglz is Java/JVM only — any remaining JS surface needs a separate flag client
Multivariate flags map poorly; redesign before cutover or keep them on the old provider
Custom targeting rules must be reimplemented as Java ActivationStrategy classes
Togglz releases are infrequent; check version compatibility with your Spring Boot baseline
Vercel-preview-based experimentation is lost; rebuild via your own analytics if needed
Admin-console authentication must be configured before exposing in production

email.subject: Migrating from Flags SDK to Togglz
email.body:
Hi team,

With the platform shifting to Java/Spring, I'm proposing we replace Vercel's Flags SDK (and the provider behind it) with Togglz inside the JVM services.

Why: type-safe enum flags, in-process evaluation, built-in admin console, no SaaS provider in the path.

Steps:
1. Export flag rules from the current provider behind Flags SDK.
2. Add Togglz to the Spring Boot app and pick a StateRepository (JDBC default).
3. Recreate each flag as an enum constant with the appropriate ActivationStrategy.
4. Run both side-by-side until parity is confirmed, then retire Flags SDK.

Caveats: multivariate flags need a redesign, JS surfaces still need their own client, and Vercel-preview A/B tests don't carry over. Effort: 2-5 days.
